pi_items.source: Verified by reviewing setup_task.sh which explicitly deploys the commons-lang3 JAR using a PUT request to the path example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar via the Artifactory REST API.
Derived from the verified target_artifact_path deployment directory in setup_task.sh.

Verified by reviewing setup_task.sh which explicitly deploys the commons-io JAR using a PUT request to the path example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/commons-io-2.15.1.jar via the Artifactory REST API.
Verified in setup_artifactory.sh which defines ARTIFACTORY_URL as http://localhost:8082 and uses it to configure and launch Artifactory.
Verified in setup_artifactory.sh which sets ADMIN_USER='admin' and configures this as the admin username via REST API.
Verified in setup_artifactory.sh which sets ADMIN_PASS='password' and configures this as the default admin password.

privileged_info_summary: The environment runs Artifactory at http://localhost:8082 with credentials admin/password. The target artifact to be deleted is deployed to example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar, and its folder is example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/. Another artifact that must be preserved is deployed to example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/commons-io-2.15.1.jar. The artifacts used in the task are real open-source Java packages downloaded from repo1.maven.org.
